question.text: Under REIT Regulations, failure to make an IPO within how many years of registration (extendable by SEBI) results in surrender of the registration certificate?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under REIT Regulations, failure to do an IPO within 3 years (which can be extended for another year by SEBI) of registration shall entail surrender of the Registration certificate. **The Trap:** This is identical to the InvIT requirement of 3+1 years. Both frameworks have the same deadline for fund raising post-registration. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Both REITs and InvITs must raise funds within 3 years (+1 year SEBI extension) of registration, preventing shelf registrations without genuine fund-raising intent.
